states.name: Alabama
states.details: Implemented for the Class of 2017. Requires a one-half credit course in Personal Finance.

states.name: Alaska
states.details: No state-wide standalone personal finance requirement for high school graduation.

states.name: Arizona
states.details: Requires personal finance concepts to be embedded in economics or math, but no standalone course mandate.

states.name: Arkansas
states.details: Requires a standalone personal finance course. The requirement was strengthened by recent legislation.

states.name: California
states.details: AB 2927 signed in 2024. Guarantees a one-semester personal finance course starting with the Class of 2031.

states.name: Colorado
states.details: Standards are embedded in other courses; local districts make standalone decisions.

states.name: Connecticut
states.details: Passed legislation in 2023 requiring a personal finance course for the Class of 2027.

states.name: Delaware
states.details: Requires a half-credit course or equivalent integrated instruction for the Class of 2011 and beyond.

states.name: Florida
states.details: The Dorothy L. Hukill Financial Literacy Act guarantees a standalone course starting with the Class of 2027.

states.name: Georgia
states.details: Mandated a half-credit course in personal finance starting in 2024.

states.name: Hawaii
states.details: No state-wide standalone requirement. Schools offer courses as electives.

states.name: Idaho
states.details: Requires a financial literacy course integrated or as a standalone for graduation.

states.name: Illinois
states.details: Requires 9 weeks of consumer education, but not necessarily a standalone full semester course.

states.name: Indiana
states.details: SB 35 (2023) mandates a personal finance course for high school graduation (Class of 2028).

states.name: Iowa
states.details: Requires a standalone personal finance course for graduation starting with the Class of 2023.

states.name: Kansas
states.details: No standalone course required, though standards exist.

states.name: Kentucky
states.details: Requires a financial literacy course for graduation, implemented for the Class of 2024.

states.name: Louisiana
states.details: Passed a mandate in 2023 requiring personal finance for graduation.

states.name: Maine
states.details: Embedded standards, but no standalone course requirement.

states.name: Maryland
states.details: Embedded standards, but no uniform statewide standalone requirement.

states.name: Massachusetts
states.details: No statewide requirement, though legislation has been frequently debated.

states.name: Michigan
states.details: Requires a half-credit personal finance course for graduation starting with the Class of 2028.

states.name: Minnesota
states.details: Signed into law in 2023, requiring a personal finance course for graduation.

states.name: Mississippi
states.details: Requires a standalone personal finance course for graduation.

states.name: Missouri
states.details: A pioneer state. Has required a one-half credit personal finance course for graduation since 2010.

states.name: Montana
states.details: No standalone requirement for high school graduation.

states.name: Nebraska
states.details: Requires a financial literacy course for graduation starting with the Class of 2024.

states.name: Nevada
states.details: Requires a standalone personal finance course for high school graduation.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.details: Requires a standalone personal finance course starting with the Class of 2027.

states.name: New Jersey
states.details: Requires a half-credit of financial, economic, business, and entrepreneurial literacy. Not tracked as a standalone mandate by NGPF.

states.name: New Mexico
states.details: Requires personal finance as an elective option, but not a strict graduation mandate for all.

states.name: New York
states.details: Board of Regents recently moved to require personal finance for graduation.

states.name: North Carolina
states.details: Requires the EPF (Economics and Personal Finance) course for graduation starting Class of 2024.

states.name: North Dakota
states.details: No standalone mandate. Currently embedded in other subjects.

states.name: Ohio
states.details: Requires a one-half unit course in financial literacy for graduation starting Class of 2026.

states.name: Oklahoma
states.details: Requires the Passport to Financial Literacy, embedded rather than a strict standalone 1-semester course.

states.name: Oregon
states.details: Signed SB 3 in 2023, requiring a one-half credit personal finance course starting Class of 2027.

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.details: Passed Act 73 in 2023, requiring a standalone course starting Class of 2027.

states.name: Rhode Island
states.details: Requires a financial literacy course for graduation starting with the Class of 2024.

states.name: South Carolina
states.details: Requires a half-credit personal finance course starting with the Class of 2027.

states.name: South Dakota
states.details: Embedded within economics, no standalone requirement.

states.name: Tennessee
states.details: A pioneering state. Requires a standalone personal finance course for graduation since 2013.

states.name: Texas
states.details: Embedded in economics, but no standalone requirement for all graduation plans.

states.name: Utah
states.details: The gold standard pioneer. Required a half-credit standalone course since 2008 and fully funds it.

states.name: Vermont
states.details: No standalone state requirement.

states.name: Virginia
states.details: Requires a standalone Economics and Personal Finance course for graduation since 2015.

states.name: Washington
states.details: Requires districts to offer it, but it is not a state-mandated graduation requirement for students.

states.name: West Virginia
states.details: Requires a civics and personal finance course for graduation.

states.name: Wisconsin
states.details: Passed Act 60 in 2023, requiring a personal finance course for graduation starting Class of 2028.

states.name: Wyoming
states.details: No state-wide standalone personal finance requirement.
